author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter X. 14–21.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those of you who hear the word of our God, not only with willingness, but also with attention, doubtless remember our promise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Indeed the same gospel lesson has also been read to-day which was read last Lord’s day; because, having lingered over certain closely related topics, we could not discuss all that we owed to your powers of understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, what has been already said and discoursed about we do not inquire into to day, lest by continual repetitions we should be prevented from reaching what has still to be spoken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know now in the Lord’s name who is the good Shepherd, and in what way good shepherds are His members, and therefore the Shepherd is one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know who is the hireling we have to bear with; who the wolf, and the thieves, and the robbers we have to beware of; who are the sheep, and what is the door whereby both sheep and shepherd enter: how we are to understand the doorkeeper.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know also that every one who entereth not by the door is a thief and a robber, and cometh not but to steal, and to kill, and to destroy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these sayings have, as I think, been sufficiently handled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To-day we ought to tell you, as far as the Lord enables us (for Jesus Christ our Saviour hath Himself told us that He is both the Shepherd and the door, and that the good Shepherd entereth in by the door), how it is that He entereth in by Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if no one is a good shepherd but he that entereth by the door, and He Himself is preeminently the good Shepherd, and also Himself the door, I can understand it only in this way, that He entereth in by Himself to His sheep, and calleth them to follow Him, and they, going in and out, find pasture, which is to say, eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I proceed, then, without more delay.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When I seek to get into you, that is, into your heart, I preach Christ: were I preaching something else, I should be trying to climb up some other way.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ, therefore, is my gate to you: by Christ I get entrance, not to your houses, but to your hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is by Christ I enter: it is Christ in me that you have been willingly hearing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why is it you have thus willingly hearkened to Christ in me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because you are the sheep of Christ, purchased with the blood of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You acknowledge your own price, which is not paid by me, but is preached by my instrumentality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, and only He, was the buyer, who shed precious blood—the precious blood of Him who was without sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet made He precious also the blood of His own, for whom He paid the price of blood: for had He not made the blood of His own precious, it would not have been said, “Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of His saints.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also when He saith, “The good Shepherd giveth His life for the sheep,” He is not the only one who has done such a deed; and yet if those who have done so are His members, He only Himself was the doer of it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was able to do so without them, but whence had they the power apart from Him, who Himself had said, “Without me ye can do nothing”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But from the same source we can show what others also have done, for the apostle John himself, who preached the very gospel you have been hearing, has said in his epistle, “Just as Christ laid down His life for us, so ought we also to lay down our lives for the brethren.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “We ought,” he says: He made us debtors who first set the example.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the same effect it is written in a certain place, “If thou sittest down to sup at a ruler’s table, make wise observation of what is set before thee; and put to thy hand, knowing that it will be thy duty to make similar provision in turn.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know what is meant by the ruler’s table: you there find the body and blood of Christ; let him who comes to such a table be ready with similar provision.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is such similar provision?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As He laid down His life for us, so ought we also, for the edification of others, and the maintenance of the faith, to lay down our lives for the brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the same effect He said to Peter, whom He wished to make a good shepherd, not in Peter’s own person, but as a member of His body: “Peter, lovest thou me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Feed my sheep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This He did once, again, and a third time, to the disciple’s sorrow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when the Lord had questioned him as often as He judged it needful, that he who had thrice denied might thrice confess Him, and had a third time given him the charge to feed His sheep, He said to him, “When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shall be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the evangelist has explained the Lord’s meaning: “But this spake He, signifying by what death he should glorify God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Feed my sheep” applies, then, to this, that thou shouldst lay down thy life for my sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now when He saith, “As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father,” who can be ignorant of His meaning?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He knoweth the Father by Himself, and we by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He hath knowledge by Himself, we know already: that we also have knowledge by Him, we have like wise learned, for this also we have learned of Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He Himself hath said: “No one hath seen God at any time; but the only-begotten Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, He hath declared Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so by Him do we also get this knowledge, to whom He hath declared Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In another place also He saith: “No one knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any one the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As He then knoweth the Father by Himself, and we know the Father by Him; so into the sheepfold He entereth by Himself, and we by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We were saying that by Christ we have a door of entrance to you; and why?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because we preach Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We preach Christ; and therefore we enter in by the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Christ preacheth Christ, for He preacheth Himself; and so the Shepherd entereth in by Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the light shows the other things that are seen in the light, does it need some other means of being made visible itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The light, then, exhibits both other things and itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever we understand, we understand with the intellect: and how, save by the intellect, do we understand the intellect itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But does one in the same way with the bodily eye see both other things and [the eye] itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For though men see with their eyes, yet their own eyes they see not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The eye of the flesh sees other things, itself it cannot [see]: but the intellect understands itself as well other things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same way as the intellect seeth itself, so also doth Christ preach Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He preacheth Himself, and by preaching entereth into thee, He entereth into thee by Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He is the door to the Father, for there is no way of approach to the Father but by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For there is one God and one Mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many things are expressed by a word: all that I have just said, I have said, of course, by means of words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I were wishing to speak also of a word itself, how could I do so but by the use of the word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus both many things are expressed by a word, which are not the same as the word, and the word itself can only be expressed by means of the word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the Lord’s help we have been copious in illustration.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Remember, then, how the Lord Jesus Christ is both the door and the Shepherd: the door, in presenting Himself to view; the Shepherd, in entering in by Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And indeed, brethren, because He is the Shepherd, He hath given to His members to be so likewise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For both Peter, and Paul, and the other apostles were, as all good bishops are, shepherds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But none of us calleth himself the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This—the way of entrance for the sheep—He has retained as exclusively belonging to Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, Paul discharged the office of a good shepherd when he preached Christ, because he entered by the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the undisciplined sheep began to create schisms, and to set up other doors before them, not of entrance to their joint assembly, but for falling away into divisions, saying, some of them, “I am of Paul;” others, “I am of Cephas;” others, “I of Apollos;” others, “I of Christ:” terrified for those who said, “I am of Paul,”—as if calling out to the sheep, Wretched ones, whither are you going?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am not the door,—he said, “Was Paul crucified for you? or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those who said, “I am of Christ,” had found the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But of the one sheepfold and of the one Shepherd, you are now indeed being constantly reminded; for we have commended much the one sheepfold, preaching unity, that all the sheep should enter by Christ, and none of them should follow Donatus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, for what particular reason this was said by the Lord, is sufficiently apparent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was speaking among the Jews, and had been specially sent to the Jews, not for the sake of that class who were bound up in their inhuman hatred and persistently abiding in darkness, but for the sake of some in the nation whom He calls His sheep: of whom He saith, “I am not sent but to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knew them even amid the crowd of His raging foes, and foresaw them in the peace of believing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, does He mean by saying, “I am not sent but to the lost sheep of the house of Israel,” but that He exhibited His bodily presence only to the people of Israel?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not proceed Himself to the Gentiles, but sent: to the people of Israel He both sent and came in person, that those who proved despisers should receive the greater judgment, because favored also with the sight of His actual presence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Himself was there: there He chose a mother: there He wished to be conceived, to be born, to shed His blood: there are His footprints, now objects of adoration where last He stood, and whence He ascended to heaven: but to the Gentiles He only sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps some one thinks that, as He Himself came not to us, but sent, we have not heard His own voice, but only the voice of those whom He sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far from it: let such a thought be banished from your hearts; for He Himself was in those whom He sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to Paul himself whom He sent; for Paul was specially sent as an apostle to the Gentiles; and it is Paul who, terrifying them not with himself but with Him saith, “Do ye wish to receive a proof of Him who speaketh in me, that is, of Christ?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen also to the Lord Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And other sheep I have,” that is, among the Gentiles, “which are not of this fold,” that is, of the people of Israel: “them also must I bring.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, even when it is by the instrumentality of His servants, it is He and not another that bringeth them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen further: “They shall hear my voice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See here also, it is He Himself who speaks by His servants, and it is His voice that is heard in those whom He sends.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That there may be one fold, and one shepherd.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of these two flocks, as of two walls, is the corner-stone formed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus is He both door and the corner-stone: all by way of comparison, none of them literally.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I have said so before, and earnestly pressed it on your notice, and those who comprehend it are wise, yea, those who are wise do comprehend it; and yet let those who are not yet intellectually enlightened, keep hold by faith of what they cannot as yet understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ is many things metaphorically, which strictly speaking He is not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Metaphorically Christ is both a rock, and a door, and a corner-stone, and a shepherd, and a lamb, and a lion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How numerous are such similitudes, and as many more as would take too long to enumerate!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if you select the strict significations of things as you are accustomed to see them, then He is neither a rock, for He is not hard and senseless; nor a door, for no artisan made Him; nor a corner-stone, for He was not constructed by a builder; nor a shepherd, for He is no keeper of four-footed animals; nor a lion, as it ranks among the beasts of the forest; nor a lamb, as it belongs to the flock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All such, then, are by way of comparison.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is He properly?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God [God was the Word].”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what, as He appeared in human nature?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us [in us].”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear also what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore doth my Father love me,” He saith, “because I lay down my life, that I might take it again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this that He says?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore doth my Father love me:” because I die, that I may rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the “I” is uttered with special emphasis: “Because I lay down,” He saith, “I lay down my life,” “I lay down.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that “I lay down”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I lay it down.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the Jews no longer boast: they might rage, but they could have no power: let them rage as they can; if I were unwilling to lay down my life, what would all their raging effect?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By one answer of His they were prostrated in the dust: when they were asked, “Whom seek ye?” they said, “Jesus;” and on His saying to them, “I am He, they went backward, and fell to the ground.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who thus fell to the ground at one word of Christ when about to die, what will they do at the sound of His voice when coming to judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I, I,” I say, “lay down my life, that I may take it again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not the Jews boast, as if they had prevailed; He Himself laid down His life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I laid me down [to sleep],” He says [elsewhere].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know the psalm: “I laid me down and slept; and I awaked [rose up], for the Lord sustaineth me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What of that—“I lay down”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because it was my pleasure, I did so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does “I lay down” mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it not a lying down to sleep on His part, who, when He pleased, rose from the tomb as He would from a bed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He loves to give glory to the Father, that He may stir us up to glorify our Creator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in adding, “I arose, for the Lord sustaineth me;” think you there was here a kind of failing in His power, so that, while He had it in His own power to die, He had it not in His power to rise again?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, indeed, the words seem to imply when not more closely considered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I lay down to sleep;” that is, I did so, because I pleased.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And I arose:” why?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because the Lord sustaineth [will sustain] me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then? wouldst Thou not have power to rise of Thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If Thou hadst not the power, Thou wouldst not have said, “I have power to lay down my life, and I have power to take it again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, as showing that not only did the Father raise the Son, but the Son also raised Himself, hear how, in another passage in the Gospel, He saith, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the evangelist adds: “But this He spake of the temple of His body.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For only that which died was restored to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word is not mortal, His soul is not mortal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If even thine dieth not, could the Lord’s be subject to death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How can I know, thou wilt say, that mine dieth not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Slay it not thyself, and it cannot die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, thou asketh, can I slay my soul?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To say nothing meanwhile of other sins, “The mouth that lieth, slayeth the soul.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, thou sayest, can I be sure that it dieth not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the Lord Himself giving security to His servant: “Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what in the plainest terms does He say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Fear Him who hath power to slay both soul and body in hell.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here you have the fact that it dieth, and that it doth not die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is its dying?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is dying to thy flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dying, to thy flesh, is the losing of its life: dying to thy soul, is the losing of its life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The life of thy flesh is thy soul: the life of thy soul is thy God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the flesh dies in losing the soul, which is its life, so the soul dieth in losing God, who is its life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of a certainty, then, the soul is immortal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Manifestly immortal, for it liveth even when dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what the apostle said of the luxurious widow, may also be said of the soul if it has lost its God, “she is dead while she liveth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, does the Lord lay down His life [soul]?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us, brethren, inquire into this a little more carefully.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The time is not so pressing as is usual on the Lord’s day: we have leisure, and theirs will be the profit who have assembled to-day also to wait on the Word of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I lay down my life,” He says.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who lays down?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What lays He down?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word and man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not man as being flesh alone: but as man consists of flesh and soul, so, in Christ there is a complete humanity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He would not have assumed the baser part, and left the better behind, seeing that the soul of man is certainly superior to the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, then, there is entire manhood in Christ, what is Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word, I repeat, and man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the Word and man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word, soul, and flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Keep hold of that, for there has been no lack of heretics on this point also, expelled as they were some time ago from the catholic truth, but still persisting, like thieves and robbers who enter not by the door, to lay their snares around the fold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These heretics are termed Apollinarians, and have ventured to assert dogmatically that Christ is only the word and flesh, and contend that He did not assume a human soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet some of them could not deny that there was a soul in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See their intolerable absurdity and madness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They would have Him to possess an irrational soul, but deny Him a rational one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They allowed Him a mere animal, they deprived Him of a human, soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they took away Christ’s reason by losing their own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it be otherwise with us, who have been nourished and established in the catholic faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, on this occasion I would remind your Charity, that, as in former lectures, we have given you sufficient instruction against the Sabellians and Arians,—the Sabellians, who say, The Father is the same as the Son—the Arians, who say, The Father is one being, the Son is another, as if the Father and Son were not of the same substance—and also, provided you remember as you ought, against the Photinian heretics, who have asserted that Christ was mere man, and destitute of Godhead: and against the Manicheans, who maintain that He was God only without any true humanity: we may, on this occasion, in speaking about the soul, give you some instruction also in opposition to the Apollinarians, who say that our Lord Jesus Christ had no human soul, that is, a rational intelligent soul,—that soul, I mean, by which, as men, we differ from the brutes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what sense, then, did our Lord say here, “I have power to lay down my soul [life]”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who lays down his soul, and takes it again?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it as being the Word that Christ does so?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or is it the human soul He possesses that lays down and resumes its own existence?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or is it His fleshly nature that lays down its life and takes it again?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us sift each of the three questions I have suggested, and choose that which conforms to the standard of truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if we say that the Word of God laid down His soul, and took it again, we should have to fear the entrance of a wicked thought, and have it said to us: Then there was a time when that soul was separated from the Word, and a time, after His assumption of that soul, when He was without a soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see, indeed, that the Word was once without a human soul, but only so, when “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But from the time that the Word was made flesh, to dwell amongst us, and manhood was assumed by the Word, that is, our whole nature, soul and flesh, what more could His passion and death do than separate the body from the soul?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It separated not the soul from the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the Lord died, yea, because He died (for He did so for us on the cross), doubtless His flesh breathed out that which was its life: for a short time the soul forsook the flesh, although destined by its own return to raise the flesh again to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I cannot say that the soul was separated from the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said to the soul of the thief, “To-day shalt thou be with me in paradise.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He forsook not the believing soul of the robber, and did He abandon His own?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely not; but when the Lord took that of the other into His keeping, He certainly retained His own in indissoluble union.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, on the other hand, we say that the soul laid down and reassumed itself, we fall into the greatest absurdity; for what was not separated from the Word, was inseparable from itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us turn, then, to what is true and easily understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take the case of any man, who does not consist of the word and soul and flesh, but only of soul and flesh; and let us inquire how any such man lays down his life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can no ordinary man do so?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou mayest say to me: No man has power to lay down his life [soul], and to take it again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But were not a man able to lay down his life, the Apostle John would not say, “As Christ laid down his life for us, even so ought we also to lay down our lives for the brethren.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore may we also (if only we are filled with His courage, for without Him we can do nothing) lay down our lives for the brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When some holy martyr has laid down his life for the brethren, who laid it down, and what laid he down?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we understand this, we shall perceive in what sense it was said by Christ, “I have power to lay down my life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Art thou prepared, O man, to die for Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am prepared, he replies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let me repeat the question in other words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Art thou prepared to lay down thy life for Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to these words he makes me the same reply, I am prepared, as he had, when I said, Art thou prepared to die?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To lay down one’s life [soul], is, then, the same as to die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in whose behalf is the sacrifice in this case?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all men, when they die, lay down their life; but it is not all who lay it down for Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And no one has power to resume what he has laid down.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Christ both laid it down for us, and did so when it pleased Him; and when it pleased Him, He took it again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To lay down one’s soul then, is to die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As also the Apostle Peter said to the Lord: “I will lay down my life [soul] for Thy sake;” that is, I will die for Thy sake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: View it, then, as referable to the flesh: the flesh layeth down its life, and the flesh taketh it again; not, indeed, the flesh by its own power, but by the power of Him that inhabiteth it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The flesh, then, layeth down its life in expiring.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look at the Lord Himself on the cross: He said, “I thirst:” those who were present dipped a sponge in vinegar, fastened it to a reed, and applied it to His mouth; then, having received it, He said, “It is finished;” meaning, All is fulfilled which had been prophesied regarding me as, prior to my death, still in the future.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because He had the power, when He pleased, to lay down His life, after He had said, “It is finished,” what adds the evangelist?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And He bowed His head, and gave up the spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is to lay down the soul [life].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Only let your Charity attend to this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He bowed His head, and gave up the spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who gave up what gave He up?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He gave up the spirit; His flesh gave it up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means, the flesh gave it up?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The flesh sent it forth, breathed it out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so, in becoming separated from the spirit, we are said to expire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as getting outside the paternal soil is to be expatriated, turning aside from the track is to deviate; so to become separated from the spirit is to expire; and that spirit is the soul [life].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when the soul quits the flesh, and the flesh remains without the soul, then is a man said to lay down his soul [his human life].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When did Christ lay down His life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When it pleased the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For sovereign authority resided in the Word; and therein lay the power to determine when the flesh should lay down its life, and when it should take it again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the flesh laid down its life, how did Christ lay down His life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the flesh is not Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly in this way, that Christ is both flesh, and soul, and the Word; and yet these three things are not three Christs, but one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask thine own human nature, and from thyself ascend to what is above thee, and which, if not yet able to be understood, can at least be believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the same way that one man is soul and body, is one Christ both the Word and man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider what I have said, and understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul and body are two things, but one man: the Word and man are two things, but one Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Apply, then, the subject to any man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where is now the Apostle Paul?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If one answer, At rest with Christ, he speaks truly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And likewise, should one reply, In the sepulchre at Rome, he is equally right.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one answer I get refers to his soul, the other to his flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet we do not say that there are two Apostle Pauls, one who rests in Christ, another who was laid in the sepulchre; although we may say that the Apostle Paul liveth in Christ, and that the same apostle lieth dead in the tomb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some one dieth, and we say, He was a good man, and faithful; he is in peace with the Lord: and then immediately, Let us attend his obsequies, and lay him in the sepulchre.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art about to bury one whom thou hadst just declared to be in peace with God; for the latter regards the soul which blooms eternally, and the other the body, which is laid down in corruption.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But while the partnership of the flesh and soul has received the name of man, the same name is now applied to either of them, singly and by itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no one, then, be perplexed, when he hears that the Lord has said, “I lay down my life, and I take it again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The flesh layeth it down, but by the power of the Word: the flesh taketh it again, but by the same power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even His own name, the Lord Christ, was applied to His flesh alone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How can you prove it? says some one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We believe of a certainty not only in God the Father, but also in Jesus Christ His Son, our only Lord: and this that I have just said contains the whole, in Jesus Christ His Son, our only Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand that the whole is here: the Word, and soul, and flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At all events thou confessest what is also held by the same faith, that thou believest in that Christ who was crucified and buried.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ergo, thou deniest not that Christ was buried; and yet it was the burial only of His flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had the soul been there, He would not have been dead: but if it was a true death, and its resurrection real, it was previously without life in the tomb; and yet it was Christ that was buried.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so the flesh apart from the soul was also Christ, for it was only the flesh that was buried.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Learn the same likewise in the words of an apostle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Let this mind,” he says, “be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus: who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who, save Christ Jesus, as respects His nature as the Word, is God with God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But look at what follows: “But emptied Himself, and took upon Him the form of a servant; being made in the likeness of men, and found in fashion as a man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is this, but the same Christ Jesus Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But here we have now all the parts, both the Word in that form of God which assumed the form of a servant, and the soul and the flesh in that form of a servant which was assumed by the form of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He humbled Himself, and became obedient unto death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now in His death, it was His flesh only that was slain by the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He said to His disciples, “Fear not them that kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul,” how could they do more in His own case than kill the body?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet in the slaying of His flesh, it was Christ that was slain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when the flesh laid down its life, Christ laid it down; and when the flesh, in order to its resurrection, assumed its life, Christ assumed it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless this was done, not by the power of the flesh, but of Him who assumed both soul and flesh, that in them these very things might receive fulfillment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This commandment,” He says, “have I received of my Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word received not the commandment in word, but in the only begotten Word of the Father every commandment resides.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the Son is said to receive of the Father what He possesses essentially in Himself, as it is said, “As the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself,” while the Son is Himself the life, there is no lessening of His authority, but the setting forth of His generation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father added not after-gifts as to a son whose state was imperfect at birth, but on Him whom He begat in absolute perfection He bestowed all gifts in begetting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this manner He gave Him equality with Himself, and yet begat Him not in a state of inequality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But while the Lord thus spake, for the light was shining in the darkness, and the darkness comprehended it not, “there was a dissension again created among the Jews for these sayings, and many of them said, He hath a devil, and is mad: why hear ye him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This was the thickest darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Others said, “These are not the words of him that hath a devil; can a devil open the eyes of the blind?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The eyes of such were now begun to be opened.
